# Exlplanationof the excel file & parsed data from it

## main features of the excel file

### data entry
users will enter their planned expenses and income description, amount and payment dates, on blue cells, line by line, by expense category and their subtotals.

### populating the main table
once each expense and its payment date are included in the expenses column and date column, excel will send a message on a table where each column represents one day of the month, and each line represents such expense, "pay R$ XXX" on the column of the day and line of the expense.

### reading the main table 
due to the sometimes large number of expenses the table will have a large number of lines and 31 columns (one for each day of the month). By allowing a minimum size of the columns so that the user can clearly read the messages "pay R$ XXX", the user will be able to scroll the table horizontally to see all the expenses at their payment dates (in the corresponding column/day of the month).

### the summary table
because most of the cells in such table are empty, and the table is large, a second table is generated by excel, where it allocates all expenses of each day of the month, with their corresponding descriptions on a contiguous column in contiguous lines, much like a calendar, where each day of the month is a box with two columns and a fixed number of lines, let´s say 10 to 15 lines (some will still be empy, and all will be empty in case there is no payment due on that day).

## the order of data entry and percentages of expenses paid
the first column of the data entry is for the description of the expense, the second column is for the amount of the planned expense, the third column is for the payment due date of the expense, the fourth column is for the payment amount of the expense, the fifth column is for the date the expense was paid, the sixth column is for the percentage paid up to the date the user is currently on. Such percentage only appears after the due date of the expense or whether the user has paid in advance of the due date of the expense. In case all amount is paid, the cell will be colored green, showing 100%. In case the user has not paid until the current date, ou paid less then 100%, the cell will be colored red,showing the percentage paid up to the date the user is currently on.

### income entry
at the bottom of the input table, the planned income for the month is included, and this amount is divided by the number of net income days of the month, to generate a daily income amount, that will populate the same line of the main table, day by day. Thre is a feature that allows the user to enter the percentages of the daily income amount on saturdays and sundays, so that excel calculates distributes the total expectes income for the month already considering the percentages of the dily income on saturdays and sundays. In case the income amount on saturdays and sundays is only released by credit cards on mondays, the user can enter Y or N on a cell to indicate whether the income amount on saturdays and sundays is only released be credit cards on mondays, so that the cells of the mais table will be empty on weekends, and the cash flow will more closely represent the real cash flow of the month, when excel adds the income and subtracts expenses on the last lines of the table. 

### the cash flows calculated
once the user enters the real amounts and dates of expenses paid, and the real amounts and dates of income received, excel can calculate the cash flow of each day of the month, and show it on the last lines of the table. 

### the graphical representations
The final result is a graphical representation, by a bar chart that shows for each day of the month the expected expenses comared to paid expenses, the expected income compared to received income, and the expectes balance compared to the real balance, in a cumulative way, i.e., each day having six bars, two for expenses, two for income and two for balance, in different colors, but same colors, light and dark for each couple of compared bars. A seond graph is also generated, showing stacked bars day by day of the month, with the stacks representing the different expenses on that day, and the colors of the stacks are the same for each category of expenses.

### late payments from past months
the user will use day -1 to enter the late payments from past months, and the app will include such late payments on the first working day of the month.

### descriptions of future expenses to keep in mind
the user will use day 0 to enter the descriptions of future expenses to keep in mind, and the app will not include them on the main table, but will show them separately on a list of future expenses to keep in mind, with the amount and the payment date.

## Parsed Excel Data

1. Sheet names
2. Excel tables (group of nearby cells acting as a table) in each sheet
3. Cell values and formulas for each excel table
4. Map cell dependencies
5. Understand conditional formatting, data validation, etc

### Data

**File:** `excel_app.xlsx`

## Sheet Overview

1. **DESP_JANEIRO** - Dimensions: A1:BL258 (258 rows x 64 columns)
2. **PLAN2** - Dimensions: A1:BD172 (172 rows x 56 columns)
3. **TUTORIAL** - Dimensions: A1:L116 (116 rows x 12 columns)

---
